headline: Apes Rise Again: Matt Shakman Swings Into New Franchise Entry
lede: Fresh off his Marvel debut, director Matt Shakman is pivoting to another beloved sci-fi franchise with a new 'Planet of the Apes' installment at 20th Century Studios.
body:
## Another Primate Saga Takes Shape

Matt Shakman, who recently navigated Marvel's superhero landscape with 'Fantastic Four: First Steps', is now charting a course for the long-running 'Planet of the Apes' franchise. The veteran director brings substantial genre credibility after successfully launching Disney's latest superhero ensemble.

### Creative Team Takes Shape

Screenwriter Josh Friedman joins Shakman in developing the next chapter of the simian saga. The pair represent a potent creative combination, with proven track records in complex world-building and narrative innovation.

### Franchise Legacy

The 'Planet of the Apes' series has consistently reinvented itself across multiple decades, from Charlton Heston's iconic 1968 original through the recent critically acclaimed reboot trilogy starring Andy Serkis. Shakman's involvement signals another potential evolutionary leap for the storied science fiction property.

Studio executives are keeping narrative details tightly under wraps, but industry insiders anticipate a fresh perspective that respects the franchise's philosophical underpinnings while introducing contemporary thematic elements.
key_facts: Matt Shakman directing new Planet of the Apes film
Follows recent critically successful reboot trilogy
